meaning: vi.蹒跚地走,摇摇晃晃地走n.蹒跚,摇晃的脚步,拖沓的脚步
examples: Zombies shamble up the rows of the grid toward your house, and if they get past your defenses, well, you know.
Once again, as when we were small, we believe that magic can happen, that we can be different, that life is not just a predictable and frequently humiliating shamble towards the grave.
Between video games, television, movies, and even fiction the undead menace continues to shamble its way through our lives or at least through mine.
